notes: Round 5 plan: pre-writing consolidation experiments addressing R4-identified limitations. Writing gate already met (go_write=true in r4_writing_gate.json). Paper has pivoted to TWO-CONTRIBUTION structure: (1) EDA regime-specific detector + (2) three-subtype taxonomy. All previous tasks (R1-R4: 22/22) are COMPLETE. All experiments are training-free. GPU tasks require 16GB+ VRAM. R5 focuses on strengthening the two primary contributions before writing phase.

tasks.name: R5-A: ITAC on Real Text Activations — Final H5 Verdict
tasks.description: Run ITAC correction on real text activations (GPT-2 Small, Wikitext-2, 10,000 tokens) to conclusively dismiss or rescue H5. R4D was skipped (marked optional) and R3 ITAC used synthetic activations only. Pipeline: (1) Load GPT-2 Small + gpt2-small-res-jb SAE layer 6 (AUROC=0.650 confirmed). (2) Cache residual stream activations on 10,000-token Wikitext-2 subset. (3) Identify late-absorbed latents from Phase 2 taxonomy (use GPT-2 L6 analogues or confirm late-absorbed set via first-letter probe). (4) For each late-absorbed latent: find parent-positive activation windows (n_pos >= 10 windows). (5) Measure FN rate before ITAC on those windows. (6) Apply ITAC correction and measure FN rate after. (7) Null test: ITAC effect on early-absorbed latents (expected: no change). (8) FVU constraint: reconstruction error change < +5%. Decision: FN reduction > 10% relative on late-absorbed → restore ITAC as minor contribution; < 5% → confirm H5 falsification extends to real activations.

pilot.pass_criteria: At least 3 late-absorbed latents evaluated with n_parent_positive >= 10 windows each on real text. FN rate computed before and after ITAC. Any positive or null FN reduction result is acceptable — both outcomes are informative. FVU constraint checked.

visualizations.description: ITAC on real activations: FN rate before/after per latent, FVU change (late-absorbed only vs. null test on early-absorbed)

tasks.name: R5-B: Three-Subtype Taxonomy Robustness — Extended SAE Configs
tasks.description: Extend three-subtype taxonomy (early/late/diffuse) to 2 additional Gemma Scope SAE configs: L5-16k and L19-16k. Current taxonomy rests on only 2 configs (L12-16k n=16, L12-65k n=65), which reviewers will flag as underpowered for the 75% early-dominance claim. Pipeline: (1) Load Gemma Scope L5-16k and L19-16k SAE weights (accessible without model access). (2) Use Neuronpedia proxy labels to identify absorbed vs. non-absorbed latents per config. (3) Apply weight-only taxonomy criterion: early if max_{k} cos(d_k, parent_probe) < tau; use parent_probe from nearest absorbed latent decoder column. (4) Run at tau = {0.2, 0.3, 0.4} for threshold sensitivity. (5) Compute early/late/diffuse fractions per config. (6) KW test for EDA ordering across 3 subtypes. (7) Aggregate across all 4 configs (L5-16k, L12-16k, L12-65k, L19-16k) to strengthen early-dominance claim. Note: SAE weights used only — no activation caching needed.

pilot.pass_criteria: Taxonomy classification runs on at least 2 new SAE configs (L5-16k and L19-16k). Early fraction computed at tau=0.3. If early fraction > 50% in both new configs, claim strengthened across 4 total configs. Threshold sensitivity table generated (tau = 0.2, 0.3, 0.4).

visualizations.description: Three-subtype taxonomy fractions across 4 SAE configs at tau={0.2, 0.3, 0.4} — extended from R3's 2-config result

tasks.name: R5-C: Controlled Dictionary Experiment — Amortization Gap vs. Sparsity Landscape
tasks.description: Hold the decoder dictionary D constant and compare absorption rates under feedforward (standard SAE encoder), OMP (Orthogonal Matching Pursuit), and 2-pass (feedforward -> zero top-K -> re-encode residual) encoding. This separates the Tang et al. (sparsity landscape causes early absorption) from O'Neill et al. (amortization gap causes early absorption) explanations. The 75% early-dominance finding motivates this test: if OMP with the same dictionary shows dramatically less early absorption, the encoder architecture is implicated in early-absorption causation. Pipeline: (1) Use Gemma Scope L12-16k SAE weights (accessible, already downloaded). (2) Generate 1,000 synthetic activations sampled from multivariate Gaussian matching d_model=2304 statistics (no model access needed). (3) Run feedforward encoding using SAE encoder weights. (4) Run OMP with K=16 (matched L0) using the same decoder D as dictionary atoms. (5) Run 2-pass encoding. (6) For each encoding: compute absorption rate for parent-child pairs identified from Phase 2 taxonomy (use decoder column directions). (7) Compare absorption rates across encoding methods. Decision: if OMP absorption rate < 50% of feedforward, encoder (amortization gap) is a major cause. If > 80%, sparsity landscape dominates.
tasks.max_batch_size_hint: N/A (synthetic activations, no GPU model required)

pilot.pass_criteria: OMP encoding runs on 100 synthetic activations with K=16. Absorption rate computed for feedforward and OMP on at least 5 parent-child pairs. Ratio (OMP absorption / feedforward absorption) computed. Any result is informative — both outcomes advance understanding of early absorption mechanism.

visualizations.description: Absorption rates under feedforward, OMP, and 2-pass encoding with the same decoder dictionary (supplementary mechanistic insight)

tasks.name: R5-D: EDA Label Quality Sensitivity Analysis
tasks.description: Characterize how sensitive EDA AUROC estimates are to label quality — addressing the limitation that Gemma proxy labels cannot be validated with direct labels (model gated). Pipeline: (1) Start from GPT-2 L6 direct labels (18 positives, ground truth, from R4A). (2) Create 5 progressively noisy label sets by randomly flipping label assignments at noise rates: 5%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 50% (10 bootstrap resamples per noise rate). (3) Compute EDA AUROC under each noise rate (1,000-bootstrap CI). (4) Identify the minimum label quality (fraction correct) needed for AUROC >= 0.65. (5) Apply this model to estimate: given the observed Gemma L12-65k AUROC = 0.468, what label quality is implied? (Already explained by class imbalance at 0.024% prevalence — document explicitly.) (6) Report sensitivity curve as Figure in Appendix. CPU-only task.

pilot.pass_criteria: AUROC computed at 3+ noise rates. Sensitivity curve generated. Minimum label quality for AUROC >= 0.65 identified. Document that class imbalance (not label noise) is the primary explanation for L12-65k failure.

visualizations.description: EDA AUROC vs. label noise rate (fraction of labels randomly flipped) — sensitivity analysis for Gemma proxy label quality limitation

tasks.name: R5 Final: Pre-Writing Consolidation and Writing Gate Confirmation
tasks.description: Aggregate all Round 5 results and confirm readiness for writing phase. CPU-only analysis task. Steps: (1) Compile updated taxonomy table across 4 SAE configs (R5-B). (2) Compile ITAC real activation result (R5-A). (3) Compile controlled dictionary experiment result (R5-C). (4) Compile label sensitivity curve (R5-D). (5) Update framing for the two-contribution paper: (a) Contribution 1: EDA as regime-specific weight-only absorption detector — AUROC 0.65-0.78 at 16k-width mid-layer SAEs; cross-model validated on Gemma + GPT-2; limitation: proxy labels for Gemma; (b) Contribution 2: three-subtype taxonomy — early ~72-75%, late ~13%, diffuse ~13%; extended across 4 configs; early-dominance finding reframes absorption mitigation. (6) Generate r5_writing_gate.json confirming go_write=true with updated evidence.

pilot.pass_criteria: r5_writing_gate.json generated. go_write=true confirmed (was already true from R4; R5 strengthens evidence). Both primary contributions have sufficient evidence for paper writing. Limitations clearly documented.

visualizations.description: Final two-contribution paper evidence summary: R3+R4+R5 evidence for each claim

parallelization_note: All R5 tasks in the first wave are fully independent and can run in parallel with 1 GPU (sequential) or faster with 2 GPUs (r5a+r5b parallel, r5c+r5d parallel). r5d is CPU-only. Wall-clock time with 1 GPU: ~3 hours; with 2 GPUs: ~1.5 hours.

fallback_decisions.if_r5a_itac_fails_to_find_late_windows: Increase to 50,000 tokens for window search. If still insufficient: confirm H5 falsification on synthetic activations (R3) is the final verdict. Report as limitation.
fallback_decisions.if_r5b_taxonomy_proxy_labels_fail: Use EDA-based clustering to approximate absorbed/non-absorbed split (threshold EDA > 0.7 as absorbed proxy). Document explicitly as approximation.
fallback_decisions.if_r5c_omp_not_implementable: Use sklearn OMP as fallback. If still failing: skip R5-C and note amortization gap test as future work.
fallback_decisions.if_no_remaining_limitations_to_address: Proceed directly to writing phase with R4 evidence. R5 tasks are strengthening experiments, not blocking.

paper_framing_locked.contribution_1: EDA as regime-specific weight-only absorption detector: AUROC 0.65-0.78 in 16k-width SAEs at layers 5-12; cross-model validation on Gemma 2B (SAE weights, proxy labels) and GPT-2 Small (direct labels); EDA=1-DecCos mathematical equivalence confirmed across 3 architectures
paper_framing_locked.contribution_2: Three-subtype taxonomy (early: ~72-75%, late: ~13%, diffuse: ~13%) revealing that early absorption (dictionary coverage failure) dominates; primary implication: wider dictionaries or hierarchy-aware training objectives are the appropriate lever for absorption reduction, not inference-time encoder fixes
negative_results: H3 cross-domain RAVEL: FALSIFIED — probe quality gates failed for all 3 hierarchies (best 59.5%, gate 85%); real absorption indistinguishable from shuffled null
H5 ITAC: FALSIFIED — 2.69% FN reduction (target 20%); structurally inapplicable to 75% early-absorbed majority
H6 scaling sign reversal: FALSIFIED — partial rho positive; insufficient L0 variation in canonical SAEs
paper_framing_locked.writing_gate_status: CONFIRMED from R4 (r4_writing_gate.json: go_write=true). R5 strengthens rather than unblocks.
